disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mechanism was an inward (inversion) twist of the ankle while stepping off a curb, about two hours ago
trigger_question_examples: How exactly did you hurt it?
Which way did your ankle roll?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I stepped off the curb and my ankle rolled inwards underneath me. It was about two hours ago.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is localised to the outer (lateral) ankle over the bony area, mild in severity
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's sore right on the outside of the ankle, over the bony bit. It's fairly mild really.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Able to bear weight immediately after the injury and walked home, limping but taking steps
trigger_question_examples: Can you stand and put weight on it?
Were you able to walk on it afterwards?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I could stand on it and walk home, just carefully with a bit of a limp.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pop or snap felt at the time, no locking of the joint, and no numbness or pins-and-needles in the foot
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel or hear a pop or snap?
Any numbness, tingling, or does the joint lock up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't feel a pop or snap, it doesn't lock, and my foot doesn't feel numb or tingly.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is anxious about a possible hidden fracture and wants to know whether an x-ray is needed
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried about?
Is there anything specific worrying you tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm really worried I've broken a bone and that I'll need an x-ray tonight.
